name: Chocolate Divine Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 pint Nonfat yogurt, or nonfat sour cream
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 12-cup Bundt pan and dust with unsweetened cocoa.
In a large bowl, sift together the flours, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt. In another bowl, mix together the granulated sugar and brown sugar until there are no lumps. Set aside.
Using the steel blade of a food processor, process the peeled potatoes until finely minced. Drain the yogurt of all excess liquid and discard it.
Add the drained yogurt, honey, vanilla, chocolate flavor, and vegetable oil to the food processor with the minced potatoes and process on high for 60 seconds. Scrape the sides of the bowl while processing.
Pour the potato mixture into the dry ingredient mixture and beat with an electric mixer on high until well combined. Scrape the sides of the bowl while beating.
In another bowl, beat the egg whites until soft peaks form. Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the batter until fully incorporated.
Pour the batter into the prepared Bundt pan. Bake in the preheated oven for 55-65 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Remove from the oven and let cool for 25 minutes. If the cake falls slightly, use a knife to go around the inside and outside edges of the cake, invert the pan over a plate, and carefully shake the cake loose.
Let the cake cool completely before frosting with chocolate glaze.
Chocolate Glaze:
Combine cocoa powder, vegetable oil, powdered sugar, and water in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil while stirring. Remove from heat and continue stirring until all the sugar is dissolved. Let cool until thick enough to pour over the cake as a glaze. If the glaze becomes too thick, reheat it until workable.
